articles.headline: India Champions New Global Green Energy Transition Pact at UN Climate Meet
articles.explainer:
• India spearheaded the 'Global Green Energy Transition Pact' at the ongoing UN Climate Change Conference in Geneva.
• The pact aims to accelerate technology transfer, financial mechanisms, and capacity building for renewable energy adoption in developing nations.
• Emphasizes a 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' approach, acknowledging historical emissions.
• Calls for developed nations to meet their $100 billion climate finance commitment and increase contributions for adaptation.
• The initiative positions India as a key leader in global climate action and sustainable development.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Right to Privacy in Data Protection Review, Cautions Against State Surveillance
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reaffirmed the fundamental right to privacy in the context of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
• While largely upholding the constitutionality of the Act, the court issued directives for stricter oversight on government access to personal data.
• Emphasized that any state surveillance must be proportionate, necessary, and backed by a clear legal framework with robust safeguards.
• Directed the establishment of an independent data protection board with greater autonomy and judicial review powers.
• The ruling is seen as a crucial step in balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights in the digital age.

articles.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by a nine-judge bench of the Supreme Court in the landmark K.S. Puttaswamy judgment in 2017.

articles.headline: India's Semiconductor Mission Sees Accelerated Investment, New Fabs Announced
articles.explainer:
• The India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) announced significant progress, with two new fabrication plants (fabs) and several assembly units receiving final approvals.
• This acceleration is attributed to revised incentive schemes and a streamlined approval process, attracting major global players.
• The new fabs are expected to generate thousands of high-skilled jobs and significantly reduce India's reliance on imported semiconductors.
• Focus areas include mature nodes for automotive and consumer electronics, along with advanced packaging technologies.
• The move is crucial for India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision and its aspiration to become a global electronics manufacturing hub.

articles.headline: Project Dolphin Gains Momentum: Focus on Habitat Restoration and Community Participation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced significant progress in 'Project Dolphin,' launched to conserve India's national aquatic animal.
• Key initiatives include habitat restoration along the Ganga and Brahmaputra river systems, focusing on reducing pollution and illegal fishing.
• Emphasis is placed on community engagement and awareness programs involving local fishermen and riverside communities.
• New research indicates a slight increase in Gangetic dolphin population in specific stretches, attributed to stricter enforcement and conservation efforts.
• The project aims for a holistic approach, integrating scientific research, policy interventions, and local participation to protect the endangered species.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in Tokyo, reaffirming commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions centered on enhancing defense cooperation, including joint military exercises and technology transfer.
• Economic agenda included boosting trade, investment in infrastructure, and cooperation in emerging technologies like AI and quantum computing.
• Both nations expressed concerns over regional security challenges and pledged to strengthen multilateral mechanisms.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan in the evolving global order.

articles.headline: National Education Policy Implementation: Review Committee Submits Interim Report
articles.explainer:
• A high-level committee formed to review the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 submitted its interim report to the Ministry of Education.
• The report highlights progress in curriculum reforms (NCF), teacher training, and integration of vocational education.
• It also points out challenges such as infrastructure gaps, digital divide, and varying implementation speeds across states.
• Recommendations include increased budgetary allocation for education, enhanced digital infrastructure, and targeted support for marginalized communities.
• The Ministry affirmed its commitment to addressing the identified gaps and accelerating the policy's transformative goals.

articles.headline: India on Track to Exceed 2030 Renewable Energy Targets: Ministry Report
articles.explainer:
• A new report from the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy indicates India is well-positioned to exceed its 2030 renewable energy capacity targets.
• The country has already achieved significant milestones in solar and wind power installations, driven by supportive policies and private sector investment.
• Focus has now shifted to grid integration, energy storage solutions, and green hydrogen production to ensure grid stability and energy security.
• International collaborations and financial mechanisms are playing a crucial role in scaling up renewable projects.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Key Module for India's First Manned Space Station
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted crucial ground tests for the life support and habitability module of its upcoming indigenous space station.
• This marks a significant milestone in India's ambitious plans to establish its own human spaceflight capability and orbital outpost by 2035.
• The module's performance under simulated space conditions met all parameters, paving the way for further integration and flight tests.
• The space station is envisioned as a platform for microgravity research, advanced material science, and astrophysical observations.
• This achievement reinforces India's growing prowess in advanced space technology and its commitment to space exploration.

articles.headline: Electoral Bond Scheme: Transparency Advocates Call for Greater Disclosure Ahead of State Elections
articles.explainer:
• With upcoming state assembly elections, civil society groups and political analysts have renewed calls for greater transparency in the Electoral Bond Scheme.
• Concerns persist regarding the anonymity of donors and its potential impact on fair elections and corporate influence in politics.
• Advocates propose reforms such as mandatory disclosure of donor identities and real-time reporting of bond purchases.
• The government maintains the scheme promotes clean money in politics, while critics argue it favors the ruling party and lacks accountability.
• The debate highlights ongoing tensions between funding political parties and ensuring democratic transparency.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Traction Globally, Several Nations Eye Adoption
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is drawing significant international interest.
• Several developing and even some developed nations are exploring adopting elements of India Stack to enhance their digital governance and financial inclusion.
• The Ministry of Electronics and IT announced collaborations with countries in Africa and Southeast Asia to share expertise and frameworks.
• This global recognition underscores India's leadership in digital innovation and its potential to contribute to global digital transformation.
• The DPI model is praised for its scalability, interoperability, and cost-effectiveness in delivering public services.

articles.headline: Indian Cities Adopt Green Infrastructure to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
articles.explainer:
• Several major Indian cities are implementing 'green infrastructure' projects to mitigate the growing urban heat island (UHI) effect.
• Initiatives include extensive tree planting, development of urban forests, cool pavements, and green roofs on buildings.
• The UHI effect leads to higher temperatures in urban areas compared to surrounding rural regions, exacerbating heatwaves and energy consumption.
• These measures aim to improve urban microclimates, enhance air quality, and boost biodiversity within city limits.
• The urban development ministries are encouraging city corporations to integrate climate-resilient planning into their master plans.

Role of urban local bodies in climate-resilient planning.

articles.imageDescription: A cityscape with green roofs and trees integrated into buildings, contrasting with a conventional concrete jungle.
articles.trivia: The difference in temperature between urban and rural areas due to the UHI effect can be several degrees Celsius, especially at night.

articles.headline: Indian Tourism Sector Shows Robust Recovery, Focus Shifts to Sustainable and Experiential Tourism
articles.explainer:
• A report by the Ministry of Tourism highlights a strong rebound in both domestic and international tourist arrivals, surpassing pre-pandemic levels.
• The recovery is driven by government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and improved connectivity, along with increased private investment.
• There's a noticeable shift towards sustainable tourism practices, eco-tourism, and experiential travel, especially in rural and lesser-known destinations.
• Challenges include infrastructure development in remote areas, skilled manpower shortages, and managing the environmental impact of increased tourism.
• The sector is crucial for job creation and boosting local economies, aligning with India's broader economic growth objectives.

• These include new highways, railway lines, and inland waterways, significantly improving connectivity within the region and with mainland India.
• The projects are vital for unlocking the economic potential of the North East, facilitating trade, and promoting tourism.
• Special emphasis is on cross-border connectivity projects with neighboring countries under the 'Act East Policy'.
• Challenges like difficult terrain, environmental concerns, and land acquisition are being addressed through innovative engineering and local stakeholder engagement.

articles.headline: INS Vikrant Deployed for Major Naval Exercise in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• India's first indigenously built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, has been deployed for a large-scale naval exercise in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• The exercise involves multiple naval assets and aims to enhance interoperability, demonstrate power projection capabilities, and ensure maritime security.
• This deployment marks a significant milestone in India's journey towards self-reliance in defense manufacturing and naval power.
• It underscores India's growing role as a net security provider in the IOR, protecting trade routes and countering piracy.
• The successful operation of INS Vikrant reinforces India's 'blue water' navy aspirations and its strategic presence.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Agricultural Export Policy to Boost Farmers' Income
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched a comprehensive new Agricultural Export Policy aimed at doubling farmers' income by 2027.
• The policy focuses on diversifying agricultural exports, promoting value-added products, and addressing supply chain bottlenecks.
• Key components include infrastructure development for post-harvest management, market intelligence, and promoting 'farm-to-port' logistics.
• It also emphasizes adherence to international quality standards and fostering organic and GI-tagged agricultural products.
• The policy seeks to integrate Indian agriculture more deeply into global value chains, ensuring better prices for farmers.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Plant and Amphibian Species in Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian researchers announced the discovery of two new species – a flowering plant and an amphibian – in the biodiversity hotspot of the Western Ghats.
• The discovery highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect these unique ecosystems from habitat loss and climate change.
• The new species were identified through extensive fieldwork and genetic analysis, contributing to India's biological inventory.
• This finding underscores the importance of continued ecological research and sustainable development practices in sensitive areas.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Services, Focus on Telemedicine and Digital Health Records
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced the expansion of its services, with a renewed focus on telemedicine and the creation of comprehensive digital health records.
• New features include improved interoperability between healthcare providers and enhanced data security protocols.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for patients to access medical consultations, prescriptions, and health records from anywhere.
• This expansion is crucial for improving healthcare accessibility, especially in rural and remote areas, and reducing the burden on physical infrastructure.
• It aligns with the government's vision of universal health coverage and leveraging technology for public welfare.

articles.headline: National Maritime Heritage Complex at Lothal Nears Completion, Boosts Cultural Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The National Maritime Heritage Complex (NMHC) at Lothal, Gujarat, is nearing completion, poised to become a major cultural and tourist attraction.
• The complex aims to showcase India's rich maritime history, from the Harappan civilization to modern times, through interactive exhibits.
• Lothal, an ancient Harappan port city, provides a historically significant location for the complex.
• The project is expected to boost local economy, create employment, and enhance cultural understanding of India's maritime prowess.
• It integrates elements of education, research, and recreation, making it a unique heritage destination.

articles.headline: BRICS Expansion: New Members to Bolster Economic Bloc, Challenges Remain
articles.explainer:
• The recent expansion of the BRICS grouping with several new member countries is expected to significantly bolster its economic and geopolitical influence.
• Analysts predict increased trade volumes, greater investment opportunities, and a stronger collective voice for the Global South.
• Challenges include integrating diverse economic systems, differing political priorities, and maintaining consensus among a larger group.
• India's role as a founding member becomes even more crucial in shaping the bloc's agenda and promoting multilateralism.

articles.headline: Food Inflation Remains a Concern Amidst Volatile Global Commodity Prices
articles.explainer:
• Despite efforts, food inflation continues to be a primary concern for policymakers, driven by volatile global commodity prices and domestic supply-side issues.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) indicated that persistent food price pressures might influence future monetary policy decisions.
• Government measures include strategic releases from buffer stocks, import duty adjustments, and monitoring of essential food item prices.
• Monsoon performance and geopolitical events continue to pose risks to agricultural output and global supply chains.
• Addressing food inflation is crucial for maintaining household budgets and overall economic stability, especially for vulnerable sections.

articles.headline: New Freshwater Fish Species Discovered in Eastern Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• Ichthyologists have identified a new species of freshwater fish in the lesser-explored regions of the Eastern Ghats.
• The discovery underscores the ecological significance of the Eastern Ghats, often overshadowed by the Western Ghats, as a reservoir of biodiversity.
• The new species belongs to a genus known for its unique adaptations to specific riverine habitats.
• Researchers call for increased conservation efforts and detailed biodiversity surveys in the Eastern Ghats to protect its fragile ecosystems.
• This finding contributes valuable data to India's aquatic biodiversity inventory and highlights the need for sustainable resource management.

articles.headline: India Eyes Space Tourism with New Policy Framework, Private Sector Participation

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Global Green Energy Transition Pact' recently in news, consider the following statements:
1. It was spearheaded by India at the UN Climate Change Conference.
2. It emphasizes a 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' (CBDR-RC) approach.
3. It primarily focuses on promoting nuclear energy technology transfer to developing nations.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: India spearheaded the 'Global Green Energy Transition Pact' at the UN Climate Change Conference. Statement 2 is correct: The pact emphasizes a 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' approach. Statement 3 is incorrect: The pact primarily aims to accelerate technology transfer, financial mechanisms, and capacity building for *renewable* energy adoption, not primarily nuclear energy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following components are considered part of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, often referred to as 'India Stack'?
1. Aadhaar
2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
3. DigiLocker
4. Goods and Services Tax Network (GSTN)
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar (identity layer), UPI (payments layer), and DigiLocker (data exchange layer) are core components of the India Stack, representing India's Digital Public Infrastructure. GSTN is a crucial digital platform for taxation but is generally not categorized as a core 'India Stack' component in the same way as Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker which form foundational, interoperable public digital goods.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Urban Heat Island (UHI) Effect:
1. It refers to urban areas experiencing higher temperatures than their surrounding rural areas.
2. Green roofs and cool pavements are strategies used to mitigate the UHI effect.
3. The UHI effect primarily occurs due to increased industrial emissions in urban centers, rather than urban infrastructure.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The UHI effect is indeed when urban areas are significantly warmer than their surrounding rural areas. Statement 2 is correct: Green roofs, cool pavements, and urban forests are all effective strategies to mitigate the UHI effect by increasing albedo and evapotranspiration. Statement 3 is incorrect: While industrial emissions can contribute to urban warming, the primary causes of the UHI effect are the absorption of solar radiation by dark surfaces (concrete, asphalt), lack of vegetation, and anthropogenic heat from buildings and vehicles, rather than solely industrial emissions.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about Project Dolphin is/are correct?
1. It is aimed at the conservation of India's National Aquatic Animal.
2. It focuses on habitat restoration along the Ganga and Brahmaputra river systems.
3. The Gangetic dolphin is listed as 'Critically Endangered' by the IUCN.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Project Dolphin is launched for the conservation of the Gangetic Dolphin, which is India's National Aquatic Animal. Statement 2 is correct: The project focuses on habitat restoration in the Ganga and Brahmaputra river systems. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Gangetic dolphin is listed as 'Endangered' by the IUCN, not 'Critically Endangered'.

prelimsMocks.question:

2. Its primary objective is to showcase India's maritime history from ancient to modern times.
3. The complex is being built with significant financial assistance from the United Nations.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The NMHC is indeed being developed at Lothal, Gujarat, a significant Harappan site. Statement 3 is incorrect: While international collaborations are possible for heritage projects, there's no specific information in the context provided that it is being built with significant financial assistance from the United Nations; it's primarily a national project.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Right to Privacy in Data Protection Review, Cautions Against State Surveillance
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reaffirmed the fundamental right to privacy in the context of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
• While largely upholding the constitutionality of the Act, the court issued directives for stricter oversight on government access to personal data.
• Emphasized that any state surveillance must be proportionate, necessary, and backed by a clear legal framework with robust safeguards.
• Directed the establishment of an independent data protection board with greater autonomy and judicial review powers.
• The ruling is seen as a crucial step in balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights in the digital age.

GS2.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Traction Globally, Several Nations Eye Adoption
GS2.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is drawing significant international interest.
• Several developing and even some developed nations are exploring adopting elements of India Stack to enhance their digital governance and financial inclusion.
• The Ministry of Electronics and IT announced collaborations with countries in Africa and Southeast Asia to share expertise and frameworks.
• This global recognition underscores India's leadership in digital innovation and its potential to contribute to global digital transformation.
• The DPI model is praised for its scalability, interoperability, and cost-effectiveness in delivering public services.

Evaluate India's position as a global leader in digital innovation and its contributions to building a more inclusive digital world.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various digital services like UPI and Aadhaar linked together.
GS2.trivia: UPI transactions crossed 10 billion per month in 2023, making it one of the world's largest real-time payment systems.

GS2.headline: G20 Health Working Group Report Recommends Enhanced Global Health Architecture
GS2.explainer:
• The G20 Health Working Group released its final report, emphasizing the need for a more resilient and equitable global health architecture.
• Recommendations include strengthening pandemic preparedness, increasing vaccine equity, and investing in primary healthcare systems worldwide.
• The report calls for greater coordination among international organizations like WHO and enhanced data sharing mechanisms.
• India played a key role in drafting sections on digital health solutions and traditional medicine integration.
• The findings will be crucial for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit and future global health policy formulations.

Evaluate the role of international cooperation and multilateral organizations in addressing future health crises and ensuring global health security.
GS2.imageDescription: A globe surrounded by medical symbols, representing global health cooperation.
GS2.trivia: India held the G20 presidency in 2023, focusing on 'One Earth, One Family, One Future'.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Services, Focus on Telemedicine and Digital Health Records
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced the expansion of its services, with a renewed focus on telemedicine and the creation of comprehensive digital health records.
• New features include improved interoperability between healthcare providers and enhanced data security protocols.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for patients to access medical consultations, prescriptions, and health records from anywhere.
• This expansion is crucial for improving healthcare accessibility, especially in rural and remote areas, and reducing the burden on physical infrastructure.
• It aligns with the government's vision of universal health coverage and leveraging technology for public welfare.

GS2.headline: BRICS Expansion: New Members to Bolster Economic Bloc, Challenges Remain
GS2.explainer:
• The recent expansion of the BRICS grouping with several new member countries is expected to significantly bolster its economic and geopolitical influence.
• Analysts predict increased trade volumes, greater investment opportunities, and a stronger collective voice for the Global South.
• Challenges include integrating diverse economic systems, differing political priorities, and maintaining consensus among a larger group.
• India's role as a founding member becomes even more crucial in shaping the bloc's agenda and promoting multilateralism.
• The expanded BRICS aims to offer an alternative to traditional Western-dominated financial and political institutions.

GS3.headline: India's Semiconductor Mission Sees Accelerated Investment, New Fabs Announced
GS3.explainer:
• The India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) announced significant progress, with two new fabrication plants (fabs) and several assembly units receiving final approvals.
• This acceleration is attributed to revised incentive schemes and a streamlined approval process, attracting major global players.
• The new fabs are expected to generate thousands of high-skilled jobs and significantly reduce India's reliance on imported semiconductors.
• Focus areas include mature nodes for automotive and consumer electronics, along with advanced packaging technologies.
• The move is crucial for India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision and its aspiration to become a global electronics manufacturing hub.

GS3.headline: Project Dolphin Gains Momentum: Focus on Habitat Restoration and Community Participation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced significant progress in 'Project Dolphin,' launched to conserve India's national aquatic animal.
• Key initiatives include habitat restoration along the Ganga and Brahmaputra river systems, focusing on reducing pollution and illegal fishing.
• Emphasis is placed on community engagement and awareness programs involving local fishermen and riverside communities.
• New research indicates a slight increase in Gangetic dolphin population in specific stretches, attributed to stricter enforcement and conservation efforts.
• The project aims for a holistic approach, integrating scientific research, policy interventions, and local participation to protect the endangered species.

GS3.trivia: The Gangetic Dolphin is one of the four freshwater dolphin species in the world, and it is functionally blind, relying on echolocation to navigate and hunt.

GS3.headline: India on Track to Exceed 2030 Renewable Energy Targets: Ministry Report
GS3.explainer:
• A new report from the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy indicates India is well-positioned to exceed its 2030 renewable energy capacity targets.
• The country has already achieved significant milestones in solar and wind power installations, driven by supportive policies and private sector investment.
• Focus has now shifted to grid integration, energy storage solutions, and green hydrogen production to ensure grid stability and energy security.
• International collaborations and financial mechanisms are playing a crucial role in scaling up renewable projects.
• This progress strengthens India's commitment to climate action and its transition towards a low-carbon economy.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Key Module for India's First Manned Space Station
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted crucial ground tests for the life support and habitability module of its upcoming indigenous space station.
• This marks a significant milestone in India's ambitious plans to establish its own human spaceflight capability and orbital outpost by 2035.
• The module's performance under simulated space conditions met all parameters, paving the way for further integration and flight tests.
• The space station is envisioned as a platform for microgravity research, advanced material science, and astrophysical observations.
• This achievement reinforces India's growing prowess in advanced space technology and its commitment to space exploration.

GS3.headline: Indian Cities Adopt Green Infrastructure to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
GS3.explainer:
• Several major Indian cities are implementing 'green infrastructure' projects to mitigate the growing urban heat island (UHI) effect.
• Initiatives include extensive tree planting, development of urban forests, cool pavements, and green roofs on buildings.
• The UHI effect leads to higher temperatures in urban areas compared to surrounding rural regions, exacerbating heatwaves and energy consumption.
• These measures aim to improve urban microclimates, enhance air quality, and boost biodiversity within city limits.
• The urban development ministries are encouraging city corporations to integrate climate-resilient planning into their master plans.

GS3.imageDescription: A cityscape with green roofs and trees integrated into buildings, contrasting with a conventional concrete jungle.
GS3.trivia: The difference in temperature between urban and rural areas due to the UHI effect can be several degrees Celsius, especially at night.

GS3.headline: Indian Tourism Sector Shows Robust Recovery, Focus Shifts to Sustainable and Experiential Tourism
GS3.explainer:
• A report by the Ministry of Tourism highlights a strong rebound in both domestic and international tourist arrivals, surpassing pre-pandemic levels.
• The recovery is driven by government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and improved connectivity, along with increased private investment.
• There's a noticeable shift towards sustainable tourism practices, eco-tourism, and experiential travel, especially in rural and lesser-known destinations.
• Challenges include infrastructure development in remote areas, skilled manpower shortages, and managing the environmental impact of increased tourism.
• The sector is crucial for job creation and boosting local economies, aligning with India's broader economic growth objectives.

GS3.headline: Mega Infrastructure Projects Bolster Connectivity in India's North-Eastern Region
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (DoNER) announced accelerated completion of several key infrastructure projects.
• These include new highways, railway lines, and inland waterways, significantly improving connectivity within the region and with mainland India.
• The projects are vital for unlocking the economic potential of the North East, facilitating trade, and promoting tourism.
• Special emphasis is on cross-border connectivity projects with neighboring countries under the 'Act East Policy'.
• Challenges like difficult terrain, environmental concerns, and land acquisition are being addressed through innovative engineering and local stakeholder engagement.

GS3.headline: INS Vikrant Deployed for Major Naval Exercise in Indian Ocean Region
GS3.explainer:
• India's first indigenously built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, has been deployed for a large-scale naval exercise in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• The exercise involves multiple naval assets and aims to enhance interoperability, demonstrate power projection capabilities, and ensure maritime security.
• This deployment marks a significant milestone in India's journey towards self-reliance in defense manufacturing and naval power.
• It underscores India's growing role as a net security provider in the IOR, protecting trade routes and countering piracy.
• The successful operation of INS Vikrant reinforces India's 'blue water' navy aspirations and its strategic presence.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Agricultural Export Policy to Boost Farmers' Income
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched a comprehensive new Agricultural Export Policy aimed at doubling farmers' income by 2027.
• The policy focuses on diversifying agricultural exports, promoting value-added products, and addressing supply chain bottlenecks.
• Key components include infrastructure development for post-harvest management, market intelligence, and promoting 'farm-to-port' logistics.
• It also emphasizes adherence to international quality standards and fostering organic and GI-tagged agricultural products.
• The policy seeks to integrate Indian agriculture more deeply into global value chains, ensuring better prices for farmers.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Plant and Amphibian Species in Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian researchers announced the discovery of two new species – a flowering plant and an amphibian – in the biodiversity hotspot of the Western Ghats.
• The discovery highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect these unique ecosystems from habitat loss and climate change.
• The new species were identified through extensive fieldwork and genetic analysis, contributing to India's biological inventory.
• This finding underscores the importance of continued ecological research and sustainable development practices in sensitive areas.

GS3.headline: Food Inflation Remains a Concern Amidst Volatile Global Commodity Prices
GS3.explainer:
• Despite efforts, food inflation continues to be a primary concern for policymakers, driven by volatile global commodity prices and domestic supply-side issues.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) indicated that persistent food price pressures might influence future monetary policy decisions.
• Government measures include strategic releases from buffer stocks, import duty adjustments, and monitoring of essential food item prices.
• Monsoon performance and geopolitical events continue to pose risks to agricultural output and global supply chains.
• Addressing food inflation is crucial for maintaining household budgets and overall economic stability, especially for vulnerable sections.

• The discovery underscores the ecological significance of the Eastern Ghats, often overshadowed by the Western Ghats, as a reservoir of biodiversity.
• The new species belongs to a genus known for its unique adaptations to specific riverine habitats.
• Researchers call for increased conservation efforts and detailed biodiversity surveys in the Eastern Ghats to protect its fragile ecosystems.

GS3.headline: India Eyes Space Tourism with New Policy Framework, Private Sector Participation
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Space is reportedly drafting a new policy framework to facilitate private sector participation in India's nascent space tourism industry.
• The policy aims to leverage ISRO's technological advancements and encourage Indian startups to develop sub-orbital and orbital tourism capabilities.
• Emphasis will be on safety regulations, environmental impact assessments, and international collaboration for space tourism ventures.
• This move aligns with India's broader strategy to liberalize its space sector and position itself as a global player in commercial space activities.
• While still in early stages, space tourism holds potential for economic growth and inspiring future generations in STEM fields.

GS3.headline: India Ranks Among Top Global Start-up Ecosystems, Focus on Deep Tech and Rural Innovation
GS3.explainer:
• A recent global report ranks India among the top three start-up ecosystems worldwide, driven by a vibrant entrepreneurial spirit and supportive government policies.
• There's a growing emphasis on 'deep tech' startups in AI, Machine Learning, and biotechnology, along with innovation in rural and social sectors.
• Government initiatives like 'Startup India' and various incubation centers have played a crucial role in fostering this growth.
• Challenges include access to early-stage funding, mentorship, and scaling up operations for global competitiveness.
• India's young demography and large domestic market provide a strong foundation for continued growth in the startup landscape.
